match.userMessage:
Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy, SCARLETT in light casual beachwear suitable for sun and sand sits at a pristine white vanity inside a bright coastal modern studio with open shutters, her fingers lifting the coral lipstick from its crisp white box and turning it slowly to catch the light, warm morning sunlight cutting sharply through the shutters to cast clean directional shadows across the vanity surface and illuminate the matte champagne-silver body of the BROADCAST_MIC mounted before her, high-key airy atmosphere with golden hour warmth, shallow depth of field softly blurring the background shutters and walls while keeping the lipstick and hands crisp, eye-level cinematic framing with true-to-life color grading and slight warmth in highlights, clean natural window light and minimal processing for an effortless fresh mood

  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 2 [LOCATION REF]: INT. BONDI STUDIO - MORNING - Bright studio space with white vanity and open shutters letting sunlight stream in
    - Image 3 [ELEMENT REF]: BROADCAST_MIC - A large-diaphragm condenser studio microphone with a matte champagne/silver metallic cylindrical body
    
    IMPORTANT: Reference images CARRY the visual identity of their labeled objects. When the prompt names an UPPERCASE element token, render it faithfully from its ELEMENT REF image — do not generate a new version from the prose. When the prompt names a LOCATION REF, use that image for the environment, lighting, and architectural identity. Prose describes how things are framed, lit, and positioned in the shot — it does not redefine what the reference already shows.
  </reference-images>
